title: Low contact clamp for non-invasive process fluid temperature indication
abstract: A process fluid temperature estimation system (200) includes a sensor capsule (206) having a temperature sensitive element (254) disposed therein configured to sense an external surface (116) of a process pipe (100). The process fluid temperature estimation system (200) includes measurement circuitry (228) coupled to the sensor capsule (206) and configured to detect a characteristic of the at least one temperature sensitive element (254) that varies with temperature and provide sensor capsule temperature information. A controller (222) is coupled to the measurement circuitry (228) and is configured to obtain a reference temperature and employ a heat transfer calculation with the reference temperature and the sensor capsule temperature information to generate an estimated process temperature output. The process fluid temperature estimation system (200) includes a mounting assembly (302) configured to mount the process fluid temperature estimation system (200) to the external surface of the process pipe (100), wherein a portion of the mounting assembly (302) is offset from the external surface (116) of the process pipe (100).
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A process fluid temperature estimation system comprising: process fluid temperature estimation system comprising: a sensor capsule having at least one temperature sensitive element disposed therein configured to sense an external surface of a process pipe; measurement circuitry coupled to the sensor capsule and configured to detect a characteristic of the at least one temperature sensitive element that varies with temperature and provide sensor capsule temperature information; and a controller coupled to the measurement circuitry, the controller being configured to obtain a reference temperature and employ a heat transfer calculation with the reference temperature and the sensor capsule temperature information to generate an estimated process temperature output; and a mounting assembly configured to mount the process fluid temperature estimation system to the external surface of the process pipe, wherein a portion of the mounting assembly is offset from the external surface of the process pipe. 2. The process fluid temperature estimation system of claim 1 wherein the mounting assembly comprises a clamp body that is offset from the external surface of the process pipe. 3. The process fluid temperature estimation system of claim 1 further comprising a standoff configured to offset the clamp body from the external surface of the process pipe. 4. The process fluid temperature estimation system of claim 3 , wherein the standoff comprises a threaded portion configured to engage a corresponding threaded portion of the clamp body. 5. The process fluid temperature estimation system of claim 4, wherein the standoff comprises a user actuatable portion that, when actuated, adjusts an offset distance of the clamp body. 6. The process fluid temperature estimation system of claim 3, wherein the standoff comprises a plurality of posts coupled to the clamp body for contacting the external surface of the process pipe. 7. The process fluid temperature estimation system of claim 3 , wherein the standoff comprises a contact portion configured to contact the external surface of the process pipe, the contact portion comprising a different material than another portion of the standoff. 8. The process fluid temperature estimation system of claim 2, wherein the clamp body comprises two or more interlocking pieces configured to be coupled together on an installed 

 process pipe, such that a flow of the process pipe continues during coupling of the two or more interlocking pieces. 9. The process fluid temperature estimation system of claim 1, further comprising insulation disposed between a portion of the mounting assembly and the process pipe. 10. The process fluid temperature estimation system of claim 9, wherein the insulation is disposed around the sensor capsule, insulating the sensor capsule to the external surface of the process pipe. 11. The process fluid temperature estimation system of claim 2, wherein the clamp body comprises an aperture configured to receive and couple to the sensor capsule. 12. A clamp for a process fluid temperature estimation system comprising: a sensor assembly receiving mechanism configured to couple to a sensor assembly; a process pipe coupling mechanism configured to couple the clamp to a process pipe, such that a portion of the clamp is offset a distance from the process pipe. 13. The clamp of claim 12, wherein the process pipe coupling mechanism comprises: a plurality of standoffs configured to couple the portion of the clamp at the offset distance from the process pipe. 14. The clamp of claim 13, wherein the process pipe coupling mechanism comprises: a plurality of threaded apertures in a body of the clamp; and wherein the plurality of standoffs couple to the clamp utilizing the plurality of threaded apertures. 15. The clamp of claim 13, wherein each of the plurality of standoffs comprises: a contact portion configured to contact the process pipe; and a clamp portion configured to couple to the clamp 16. The clamp of claim 15, wherein the contact portion comprises a different material than the clamp portion. 17. The clamp of claim 12, further comprising insulation configured to be disposed between the clamp and the process pipe. 18. The clamp of claim 12 wherein the sensor assembly receiving mechanism comprises a threaded aperture configured to receive a corresponding threaded portion of the sensor assembly. 19. A method of estimating process fluid temperature within a conduit, the method comprising: coupling a mounting assembly to the conduit, the mounting assembly comprising a clamp configured to couple to the conduit at a standoff distance; coupling a sensing assembly to the conduit; sensing, with the sensing assembly, a skin temperature of an outer surface of the conduit; obtaining a reference temperature; and determining a temperature of process fluid within the conduit based on the skin temperature, reference temperature, and known thermal conductivity of the conduit. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein coupling the mounting assembly to the conduit comprises: aligning a first clamp portion with a second clamp portion; and mating a first mating feature of the first clamp portion with a second mating feature of the second clamp portion. 21. The method of claim 19, wherein coupling the mounting assembly to the conduit comprises: aligning a first clamp portion with a second clamp portion; and mating a male mating feature of the first clamp portion with a female mating feature of the second clamp portion. 22. The method of claim 19, wherein obtaining the reference temperature includes receiving process communication indicative of the reference temperature.
